response_raw:
{
    "mode": "human",
    "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
    "opinion": "As a self-employed electrician in Los Angeles with two kids and a busy life, I don't have much time for social media—just checking in a few times monthly to see what's up with friends or catch some football highlights. Honestly, fake content doesn't really get to me because I'm not on there enough to let it mess with my head. When I do scroll through, I keep it simple: if something looks off or too wild to be true, I just skip it. I've got real-world stuff to handle, like wiring jobs and taking care of my family, so I don't waste energy on online drama. My approach is practical—I trust my gut and focus on what I know is real, like my work and hobbies like gardening or playing videogames with my kids. Social media is just a tool, not my life, so I don't let fake posts stress me out; I'd rather spend time traveling or enjoying a good game of calcio.",

    "tone": "Direct, pragmatic, slightly dismissive of online issues, grounded in real-life priorities",

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a self-employed electrician in Los Angeles with two kids and a busy life, I don't have much time for social media—just checking in a few times monthly to see what's up with friends or catch some football highlights. Honestly, fake content doesn't really get to me because I'm not on there enough to let it mess with my head. When I do scroll through, I keep it simple: if something looks off or too wild to be true, I just skip it. I've got real-world stuff to handle, like wiring jobs and taking care of my family, so I don't waste energy on online drama. My approach is practical—I trust my gut and focus on what I know is real, like my work and hobbies like gardening or playing videogames with my kids. Social media is just a tool, not my life, so I don't let fake posts stress me out; I'd rather spend time traveling or enjoying a good game of calcio.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: low openness (score 12) suggests skepticism toward new/unverified info, high conscientiousness (score 75) indicates practical, organized approach; high extraversion (score 97) implies social but not deeply engaged online; low agreeableness (score 5) means independent, not easily swayed; high neuroticism (score 83) suggests emotional sensitivity but managed via limited social media use; infrequent use ('few times monthly') reduces exposure; occupation (electrician) and self-employment emphasize hands-on, real-world focus; hobbies (travel, gardening, football, videogames) prioritize offline activities; age (25) and family (children:2) add responsibility, minimizing online distractions.
response_parsed.tone: Direct, pragmatic, slightly dismissive of online issues, grounded in real-life priorities
